input.headline: Why an AI 'godfather' is quitting Meta after 12 years

raw.headline: Why an AI 'godfather' is quitting Meta after 12 years

winner: After twelve years of training the AI to be all-knowing and omnipotent, the godfather is finally leaving Meta to pursue his true passion: being a regular person who can't figure out how to turn on his own toaster.

candidates.text: After 12 years of guiding Meta's AI, I'm leaving—turns out being the "godfather" means you're always on call for family drama, and no one's interested in your 3 a.m. algorithmic interventions.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on 'godfather' role, specific to AI and Meta, concise and natural with a clever play on expectations."}

candidates.text: After 12 years of trying to make humans believe in a digital god, the AI 'godfather' at Meta finally realized he wasn't creating a messiah—he was just the guy who accidentally made everyone's internet browser start reciting scripture.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the 'digital god' concept, specific reference to Meta and internet browsers reciting scripture, concise and natural language."}

candidates.text: After twelve years of training the AI to be all-knowing and omnipotent, the godfather is finally leaving Meta to pursue his true passion: being a regular person who can't figure out how to turn on his own toaster.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on AI capabilities with a relatable, specific punchline about a toaster."}

candidates.text: Why did the AI godfather leave Meta? Because he couldn't handle all the *deep learning*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline and uses a pun on 'deep learning,' but it's generic and lacks surprise or specificity. The humor is mild and not strongly tied to the context of an AI 'godfather' quitting."}

candidates.text: Why did the AI godfather quit Meta? He didn't want to work for a company that calls its algorithms "sons of Facebook.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and has a mild pun, but the humor is weak and the connection to the headline is not very strong."}

candidates.text: After twelve years of making Meta's algorithms, the AI godfather is finally moving on to a simpler life of "Deep Learning" to "Deep Retirement.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay with 'Deep Learning' and 'Deep Retirement,' and ties into the AI theme. However, it lacks some surprise and specificity compared to what could be possible."}

candidates.text: He's leaving Meta for a simpler life—no more algorithmic divinity, just a little less drama and a lot more "I'm out of here.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat humorous with the play on 'algorithmic divinity,' but it's a bit generic and lacks the punch needed for maximum impact. The phrasing could be tighter for better surprise and specificity."}

candidates.text: He’s tired of the company's data policies and is moving to the suburbs to focus on his true passion: demanding that everyone call him "Don" and making an offer they can't refuse.
He’s not leaving because he’s tired of being worshipped — he just can’t handle the constant requests for prayers.
